course_description.overview: Wales' premier road sportive featuring spectacular coastline, serene country lanes, and the challenging Preseli Hills with over 10,000 ft of climbing on the Epic 105 route.

key_segments.name: Pembrokeshire Coastline
key_segments.description: Rugged coastal roads with spectacular views.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Challenging with over 10,000 ft elevation gain, steep hills, and cut-off times enforcing pace; not for the faint-hearted.

logistics.getting_there: Start in historic St Davids; nearest major hub Haverfordwest (Dyfed area).
logistics.accommodation: Local options in St Davids and Pembrokeshire; book early due to sell-out event.
logistics.registration_details: Entry £30-£40; limited to 1600 riders, sells out every year; charity places available via partners like Cancer Research Wales.
logistics.bike_logistics: Road bikes recommended; mobile mechanical assistance provided.
logistics.local_considerations: Expect festival atmosphere; weather variable with coastal exposure; cut-offs redirect slower riders to shorter routes for safety.

final_verdict.one_liner: Iconic Welsh sportive with breathtaking scenery and brutal climbs that sells out annually.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Experienced road cyclists seeking challenging sportives with epic coastal views and hill climbs.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for its scenery, organization, and prestige as UK's most scenic sportive.
pro_tips: Train for 10,000+ ft climbing and respect cut-offs.
Consider charity entry for team support and jersey.
